title: Threat of Explosion From Toxic Chemical Tank Lessened, Officials Say
description: The risk of a large explosion has been averted, officials say. But a smaller blast remains possible, and 16,000 people remain displaced.
author: Reis Thebault, Heather Knight, Shawn Hubler and Christina Morales

title: Main Takeaways From Pope Leo’s Encyclical on A.I.
description: In his letter, the first American pontiff called for putting care for humans at the heart of technological change.
author: Ruth Graham and Elizabeth Dias

title: Clarence B. Jones, Who Helped Shape ‘I Have a Dream’ Speech, Dies at 95
description: A confidant and lawyer for Martin Luther King, he was an unseen hand behind major civil rights events, including the 1963 March on Washington.
author: Robert D. McFadden

title: Manny Nosowsky, Waggish Crossword Constructor for The New York Times, Dies at 94
description: For nearly two decades, the prolific puzzle maker, a retired urologist, charmed solvers with his penchant for witty, waggish puns.
author: Deb Amlen

title: Amid Iran War, Remembering the Losses From Another Middle East Conflict
description: On this Memorial Day, families of those killed in the Iraq War reflected on the sacrifice and offered advice to today’s military families.
author: Clarence Williams

title: After Defeat, Massie Opens Door to a 2028 Run. Which Office Is Unclear.
description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky, a critic of President Trump who lost his primary last week, filed papers for another candidacy but said he was not sure which office he might seek.
author: Michael Kruse

title: Trump Urges More Countries to Normalize Relations With Israel as Part of Iran Deal
description: President Trump called for countries in the Middle East to join the Abraham Accords as part of a peace deal with Iran. Analysts say it is unlikely.
author: Tyler Pager and Euan Ward

title: This Memorial Day Starts a Summer That Is Longer Than Most
description: A calendar quirk brings more days of lemonade, glamping and difficult-to-open beach umbrellas to 2026.
author: Victor Mather

title: A Terrible Winter for Snow Heads Into a Bleak Summer of Drought
description: In the Desert and Mountain West, towns like Kearny, Ariz., are already warning residents they could soon run out of water, but rationing so far has been patchy.
author: Jack Healy, Reis Thebault and Scott Dance

title: Were the Constitution’s Authors a Little Too Optimistic?
description: The nation’s founding document has a blind spot. Trump is making it visible.
author: Adam Liptak

title: Will Maureen Galindo’s ‘Zionists’ Comments Matter in Texas House Runoff Election?
description: Democratic leaders accused Maureen Galindo of antisemitism and are trying to stop her from winning the party’s primary in a contested U.S. House district.
author: Edgar Sandoval and J. David Goodman

title: Crack Forms in Garden Grove Chemical Tank, a Positive Sign for Firefighters
description: The tank in Southern California has been building in temperature and pressure for days, which could lead to an explosive release. The crack may reduce some of that pressure, officials said.
author: Charlotte Dulany and Shawn Hubler

title: 5 Big Moments in the Texas Republican Senate Race
description: The contest between the incumbent John Cornyn and his challenger Ken Paxton will culminate in a Tuesday runoff. Here are the developments that shaped the campaign.
author: Taylor Robinson

title: Trump’s Self-Indulgent Streak Deepens G.O.P. Fears He Is Risking Losses in Midterms
description: “The stupid stuff is killing our chances,” said a retiring Republican senator.
author: Shane Goldmacher and Patricia Mazzei

title: He Name-Drops Ocasio-Cortez in His Bid for Congress. She Doesn’t Talk About Him at All.
description: Saikat Chakrabarti, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s former chief of staff, is running in San Francisco to succeed Representative Nancy Pelosi, whom Mr. Chakrabarti irked with an incendiary tweet seven years ago.
author: Kellen Browning

title: Artificial Intelligence Floods Court Dockets with Home-Brewed Lawsuits
description: For years, courts have welcomed cases brought by self-represented litigants. Now those plaintiffs have A.I., and their filings are consuming more and more bandwidth.
author: Mattathias Schwartz and Zach Montague

title: Confusion and Worry After Trump Administration’s Abrupt Green Card Changes
description: Immigrants and their advocates and lawyers are trying to interpret a new Trump administration rule that requires people to be in their native country to apply for a green card.
author: Christina Morales

title: A Democrat Took on Red Sox Ownership in an Ad. A Network Pulled it.
description: Graham Platner, a Senate candidate in Maine, blamed private equity for destroying “our favorite baseball team.” NESN, which is owned by the team, pulled his campaign ad, citing an intellectual property violation.
author: Taylor Robinson and Tim Balk

title: What to Know About the Citizenship Lists Trump Wants to Create
description: President Trump is trying to create individual lists of citizens by state to determine who can vote, even as his administration acknowledges they would be unreliable.
author: Adam Sella

title: Bystander Wounded in White House Shooting Is in Stable Condition
description: The Metropolitan Police Department is investigating to determine who shot the bystander, who underwent surgery, and how many bullets were fired.
author: Katie Benner
